summary: Inventory every page of the current thefairytails.co.uk site + SEO baseline via Ahrefs

pages.title: The Fairy Tails K9 Centre

sections.heading: Welcome
sections.summary: Introductory hero message inviting visitors to experience dog care and training services in a safe environment.

sections.heading: Our Services
sections.summary: Overview listing the primary service categories: Dog Day School, Dog Training, and related programs.

sections.heading: Dog Day School
sections.summary: 'Doggy day care with a difference' — a safe environment for dogs to stay, learn, play and socialise, with structured activities and monthly membership options. Links to /dog-day-school.

sections.heading: Dog Training
sections.summary: Range of training programs from intensive training to boarding-school options, plus new puppy group classes. CTA to call 01424 300668.

sections.heading: Training Plans — Comprehensive Puppy Training
sections.summary: One-month intensive program for puppies up to 20 weeks including boarding, full-day school and structured socialisation. £1,200.

sections.heading: Training Plans — Intensive Dog Training
sections.summary: Eight-week TAR (Train, Adjust, Repeat) program addressing behavioural issues, with parent (owner) training included from week five. £2,000.

sections.heading: Training Plans — Dog Boarding School
sections.summary: Eight-week residential 'Board and Train' program with initial assessment, in-home training and owner participation. £2,500.

sections.heading: Training Plans — Holiday Board & Train
sections.summary: Four-week program combining boarding, training and structured day care, with daily training sessions and video reports. £1,500.

sections.heading: Training Plans — Puppy Training Classes
sections.summary: Group classes for puppies under six months, held Thursday evenings; four sessions spread over one month. £175 per course.

sections.heading: Training Plans — Dog Social Walks
sections.summary: Small group walks (maximum five dogs per group), exclusive to existing clients, held weekends with an enrichment focus. £20 per walk.

sections.heading: Training Plans — One to One Dog Training
sections.summary: Private sessions addressing anxiety and behavioural problems at the dog's individual pace. £49.99 per session.

sections.heading: Training Plans — Free Phone Consult Service Advice
sections.summary: 'Complimentary Phone Consultation' to help owners select the appropriate training program; bookable via Acuity.

sections.heading: Membership Plans — Membership Half Days
sections.summary: AM or PM half-day options for registered school dogs, including hot towel wash and monthly grooming. £275/month (+£25 large dogs).

sections.heading: Membership Plans — Membership Full Days Tier 1
sections.summary: Invoiced monthly bookings with free pickups, grooming, and one free training session per month. £375/month (+£25 large dogs).

sections.heading: Membership Plans — Membership Full Days Tier 2
sections.summary: Bi-monthly option with free full grooming and four free training sessions per cycle. £750 bi-monthly, no large-dog surcharge.

sections.heading: Membership Plans — Dog Grooming Subscription
sections.summary: Monthly subscription including one maintenance or full groom plus free nail clipping. £25/month; booked via the grooming Acuity site.

sections.heading: Canine Education Philosophy
sections.summary: Metaphorical explanation that lasting training results require both professional instruction and owner consistency.

sections.heading: Dog Grooming
sections.summary: Full grooms, massage and teeth cleaning tailored to individual breeds; prices start at £25. Points to the separate grooming site fairytailsdoggrooming.co.uk.

sections.heading: Dog Boarding
sections.summary: 'Home from home' overnight stays with day-school activities, available to existing clients. Links to /boarding-information.

sections.heading: Dog Shop
sections.summary: Adventure Dog retail partnership offering leads, harnesses, toys and grooming products with free local same-day delivery.

sections.heading: Additional Services — Training Resources
sections.summary: 'Puppy Course - DIY' available for download (links to /puppycourse), with more materials coming soon.

sections.heading: Additional Services — Doggy Events
sections.summary: Summer barbecues and Christmas parties for day-school clients and their families.

sections.heading: Additional Services — Help and Advice
sections.summary: Open invitation to contact the business about any dog-related problems (phone CTA 07842 116216).

sections.heading: About Us
sections.summary: Located in Hastings Country Park with ample parking and free local pickup/drop-off services.

sections.heading: The Fairy Tails Club
sections.summary: Comprehensive 'A to Z care service' with daily activity updates, a contact helpline and a commitment to customer satisfaction.

sections.heading: Industry Commitments
sections.summary: Membership commitments to 'pain-free dog care' and improving industry standards.

pages.title: The Fairy Tails Dog Boarding School (Residential Dog Training / Board & Train)

sections.heading: Residential Dog Training
sections.summary: Intro to the intensive board-and-train programme: dogs live with staff for several weeks of behaviour modification, positioned as a 'boot camp' style residential service.

sections.heading: A boarding school for dogs and puppies
sections.summary: Frames the service as a boarding school suitable for both puppies and adult dogs needing training.

sections.heading: Why choose The Fairy Tails?
sections.summary: Three selling points: positive-reinforcement environment (no aversive tools), staff expertise, and a no-kennels home-based care model.

sections.heading: Is it suitable for all dogs?
sections.summary: Eligibility breakdown: puppies from 12-16 weeks need ~4 weeks; adult dogs 16+ weeks need 8 weeks minimum; rescue dogs assessed case-by-case.

sections.heading: Our Limitations!
sections.summary: Honest expectations-setting: fundamental character traits (e.g. anxious/shy temperament) cannot be completely altered by training.

sections.heading: Issues we routinely deal with
sections.summary: List of ~12 behavioural problems handled, including lead pulling, reactivity, biting, crate training, and hyperactivity.

sections.heading: Training Regime
sections.summary: Week-by-week programme outline: settling in (week 1), basics (week 2), specialisation (weeks 5-7), transition (weeks 8-9), plus a mandatory break.

sections.heading: Just some of our lovely doggy clients!
sections.summary: Testimonial/client showcase carousel with four items featuring past doggy clients.

sections.heading: Boarding Dog Training Cycles
sections.summary: Two infographic images depicting the in-facility and outdoor training environments/cycles.

sections.heading: Please note - your involvement in dog training is the most important part
sections.summary: Emphasises that owner follow-through after the programme is essential for lasting results.

sections.heading: How to book your dog into the boarding school
sections.summary: Four-step booking workflow: contact, prioritise issues to work on, contract, then agreement and payment.

sections.heading: List of items required for Boarding School
sections.summary: Detailed 22-item equipment checklist owners must supply, from crates to medications/supplements (Adaptil, Fortiflora, Nutri Calm).

sections.heading: Pricing
sections.summary: Two-tier pricing: puppy programme and adult behaviour programme, with a large-dog surcharge (exact figures in pricing field).

sections.heading: You are welcome to visit your dogs whenever you like
sections.summary: Open visit policy — owners may visit their dogs at any time during the stay.

sections.heading: Training feedback
sections.summary: Daily reports plus body-cam recordings of every training session, stored on OneDrive with a shared access link for the owner. Exact copy: 'All our training sessions are recorded on the body cam; therefore, you will also receive the recording of the training sessions with your daily reports each day. Videos are stored and transferred onto one drive, and you will have access to the link.'

pricing.details: Verbatim: '£1200 for 4 weeks'. Covers toilet training, obedience, prevention, socialisation. Puppies 12-16 weeks old.
Verbatim: '£2500 for 8 to 10 weeks'. Coaching and strategy-based behaviour-modification training for adult dogs (16+ weeks).
Verbatim: '+£300 for large dogs'. Applies to the 8-10 week adult programme.

pages.title: The Fairy Tails Dog Day School

sections.heading: Navigation / Header
sections.summary: Logo plus main menu: Home, Board & Train, Training Plans, Dog Day School, Membership Plans, Dog Grooming (external domain), Contact, and a Book Appointment button (Acuity).

sections.heading: Hero Carousel
sections.summary: Five image slides with captions: Doggy Day School; love/care/happiness; friendship and fun; beautiful surroundings; located in Hastings Country Park.

sections.heading: Where Dogs Learn That Learning Is Fun
sections.summary: Introductory pitch positioning the day school as a safe place for dogs to stay, play and learn, with emphasis on socialization and enrichment.

sections.heading: Enrichment
sections.summary: Explains canine enrichment needs — feral dogs spend ~70% of the day hunting, so domestic dogs need structured stimulation; the school uses positive behaviour-modification methods.

sections.heading: A Typical Day at Dog School
sections.summary: Full daily timetable: 7:30-9:00 pick-ups; 9:00-9:30 welcome; 10:30-12:30 walking/enrichment; 12:30-2:00 lunch; 2:00-3:00 rest; 3:00-3:30 obedience and reports; 3:30-4:30 end-of-day procedures; 4:45-7:00 drop-offs.

sections.heading: Admission Criteria
sections.summary: Requires an introductory assessment plus 3 taster sessions; no intact males over 6 months (except training programs), no banned breeds, no dogs with allergies/medication/medical issues or risky behaviour; cannot accept dogs before 8am (HBC planning restriction); muzzling/isolation policy for at-risk dogs under H&S and Dangerous Dogs Act compliance.

sections.heading: Why People Choose Us
sections.summary: Four icon-based reasons: regular buddies, local pick-ups/drop-offs, strict entrance criteria, and staff who love dogs.

sections.heading: Getting Ready for Doggy Day School
sections.summary: Checklist of required items: collar with ID tag, appropriate collar, non-retractable lead, fitted harness, vaccination card, microchip, flea-treatment declaration, lunch box, blanket, plain boiled chicken treats only, signed registration form.

sections.heading: Treatment Philosophy (How We Treat Your Dog)
sections.summary: Three subsections: dogs treated as family; calm and positive atmosphere; structured day combining play, lunch, nap, obedience work and trainer involvement.

sections.heading: Day School Plans (Pricing)
sections.summary: Pricing table covering Full Day, Half Day AM, Half Day PM with large-dog surcharges and weekly-booking discounts, plus pick-up/drop-off and walking add-ons, and two monthly membership tiers.

sections.heading: Confidence Building Sessions (CBS)
sections.summary: £15 per ~30-minute session with a recorded video provided; addresses lead pulling, focus, recall, obedience and town walks; availability dependent.

sections.heading: CBS Image Gallery
sections.summary: Six-slide carousel showing CBS training scenarios: confidence building, beach training, focus work, bus travel, Day School Report and CBS Report examples.

sections.heading: View Our Gallery
sections.summary: Short section linking to the site's /gallery page.

sections.heading: Footer
sections.summary: Logo, links to Blog, Terms & Conditions, Gallery, Employee Login (/staff-resources), contact details (phone 01424 300668, email, WhatsApp), Facebook and Instagram links, and a Google Maps link.

(2) The page body itself contains no pricing; nav references Training Plans and Membership Plans as homepage anchors (/#Trainingplans, /#MembershipPlans), implying pricing lives on the homepage. (3) Booking is via Acuity Scheduling (owner=13914499). (4) Two contact identities exist: 07842 116216 (primary, with correctly formed wa.me link https://wa.me/4407842116216 — though even this has a stray leading 0 after 44) and 01424 300668 (footer) whose WhatsApp link https://wa.me/01424300668 is malformed (UK local format, not international 44...) and likely broken — flag for fix in rebuild. (7) The fetched summary said "20 blog post entries" but only 19 distinct posts/URLs were enumerated; count discrepancy may be a summarization artifact or one post may have been missed (possibly a duplicate-styled entry). pages.title: The Fairy Tails K9 Centre Terms and Conditions

sections.heading: Terms and conditions (intro) / Our quality policy
sections.summary: Opening statement of commitment to animal welfare and service quality, delivered through staff training, veterinary partnerships, ongoing knowledge development and continuous improvement.

sections.heading: For Dog Grooming terms and conditions please click below
sections.summary: Short pointer section directing grooming clients to the separate grooming site's T&Cs at fairytailsdoggrooming.co.uk/terms-and-conditions/.

sections.heading: Our dog care policy
sections.summary: Framing copy introducing the terms that govern daycare enrolment, client responsibilities, liability limits and service conditions.

sections.heading: DOG DAYCARE TERMS & CONDITIONS (the Agreement)
sections.summary: The core legal document: a ~36-clause agreement covering enrolment acceptance, guardian authority, financial responsibility, liability exemptions and cap, transportation, health/vaccination requirements and dispute resolution.

sections.heading: OPENING HOURS
sections.summary: States normal opening hours: 8am to 5.30pm Monday to Friday, excluding Bank Holidays and Public Holidays.

sections.heading: FEES
sections.summary: Fees are as displayed on the website; outside-hours services available by prior arrangement at rates agreed in advance; late collection charges apply (clause 18).

sections.heading: COLLECTED DOGS
sections.summary: Procedure for dogs not collected by closing time: contact attempts to the client/emergency contacts, with possible relocation of the dog to overnight care.

sections.heading: FORCE MAJEURE
sections.summary: Neither party is liable for delay or failure caused by circumstances beyond reasonable control.

sections.heading: ENTIRE AGREEMENT
sections.summary: The agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all previous agreements.

sections.heading: VARIATION
sections.summary: Standard boilerplate: changes to the agreement must be agreed/made per the stated variation procedure.

sections.heading: WAIVER
sections.summary: Failure to enforce a right is not a waiver of that right.

sections.heading: SEVERANCE
sections.summary: If a clause is found invalid, the remainder of the agreement stays in force.

sections.heading: NOTICES
sections.summary: How formal notices between the parties must be given.

sections.heading: THIRD PARTIES
sections.summary: No third-party rights under the agreement.

sections.heading: INTERPRETATION
sections.summary: Definitions/construction rules for reading the agreement.

sections.heading: GOVERNING LAW & JURISDICTION
sections.summary: Agreement governed by the law of England & Wales with that jurisdiction for disputes.

sections.heading: Why sometimes training a dog takes time at The Fairy Tails
sections.summary: Educational copy explaining that training duration varies and complex cases require patience from staff and owners.

sections.heading: Stages of learning and behaviour development in a dog (Acquisition / Fluency-Maintenance / Generalisation / Adaptation)
sections.summary: Four sub-sections each describing one stage of canine learning: acquiring a new behaviour, building fluency and maintaining it, generalising it to new environments, and adapting it to real-world situations.

pricing.details: Charged when a dog is not collected within normal opening hours (clause 18). Verbatim: 'a Late Collection Fee of £5 will be incurred by the client for every 30 minutes, or part thereof'.
Verbatim: 'Late payment of fees may result in an administration charge of £25...' (clause 19).

pricing.price: 5% above Bank of England Base Rate
pricing.details: Interest on outstanding amounts, in addition to the £25 admin charge (clause 19).

pricing.price: £1,000 or total fees paid, whichever is lower
pricing.details: Verbatim: 'overall liability to the Client...shall be capped at either £1,000 or the total fees paid under the Agreement, whichever amount is lower' (clause 3.3).
Rates to be agreed in advance (clause 16). Daycare fees themselves are 'as displayed on the website' — no service price list on this page.

pages.notes: Page is live, not a 404. It is a long legal/T&C page, not a blog index. Key rebuild facts: (1) Grooming is run on a SEPARATE domain (fairytailsdoggrooming.co.uk) with its own T&Cs — this page explicitly delegates grooming terms there, so the rebuild must keep daycare and grooming terms separated. (2) One unknown internal page discovered: /staff-resources (Employee Login). (3) Nav contains two homepage anchor targets (#Trainingplans, #MembershipPlans) the new homepage must preserve or redirect. (4) Both WhatsApp links are malformed (wa.me/4407842116216 keeps the leading 0; wa.me/01424300668 is a landline with no country code) — fix in rebuild to wa.me/447842116216. (5) Business data embedded in copy worth carrying over: opening hours 8am-5.30pm Mon-Fri excl. bank/public holidays; £5/30min late collection; £25 late-payment admin + 5% over BoE base rate; £1,000 liability cap; England & Wales governing law; Acuity owner ID 13914499. (6) No prices for the actual services are on this page ('fees as displayed on the website'), so pricing lives on other pages. (7) The 'Why training takes time' section (four learning stages) is marketing/educational copy oddly placed on a legal page — consider relocating it in the rebuild.

sections.heading: Hand Stripping

Its nav differs from the known list: it uses /who-we-are/ (About) and /services/ instead of /dog-boarding-school and /dog-day-school — those two known paths do not appear on this page, suggesting the known nav belongs to a sibling/newer site. No prices and no opening hours anywhere on the page; pricing presumably lives on /services/ or inside the Acuity scheduler. Footer copyright: 2026. Key rebuild assets: Acuity booking URL, Google place ID for reviews, three on-site testimonials plus four Google reviews quoted above.

seo.notes: AHREFS DATA LARGELY UNAVAILABLE. The Ahrefs MCP server is connected, but the linked Ahrefs subscription has no API v3 access: all Site Explorer endpoints (site-explorer-domain-rating, site-explorer-backlinks-stats, site-explorer-metrics, site-explorer-organic-keywords, site-explorer-top-pages) and even subscription-info-limits-and-usage returned MCP error -32001 "Insufficient plan". These endpoints require an Ahrefs plan with API units (Enterprise / API add-on). topKeywords and topPages are therefore empty, and organic traffic and referring-domain counts could not be retrieved. The ONLY datapoint obtainable was the free public domain-rating endpoint: thefairytails.co.uk DR = 0.4 as of 2026-06-11 (effectively DR 0). IMPLICATIONS FOR THE REBUILD: a DR of ~0 means the domain carries almost no backlink equity, so the ranking value (if any) is overwhelmingly from on-page content, local relevance, and branded searches rather than links — but this could not be confirmed with keyword/page data. RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS to build the redirect map without Ahrefs: (1) pull Google Search Console performance data (queries + pages, last 12-16 months) for the property — that is ground truth for which URLs and keywords actually earn impressions/clicks and is free; (2) crawl the current live site (e.g. sitemap.xml + a simple crawler) to inventory every indexed URL so each one gets a 301 to its new equivalent; (3) check Google Business Profile, since for a local dog-care business most discovery traffic is likely Maps/local pack rather than organic web. If Ahrefs data is genuinely needed, upgrade the Ahrefs plan to one with API access or export keyword/top-pages reports manually from the Ahrefs web UI.

tech.platform: Duda website builder, white-labelled/hosted through IONOS ("IONOS MyWebsite Now / Website Builder", which is Duda under the hood). Evidence: sole external runtime script https://static-cdn.website-editor.net/mnlt/production/6507/_dm/s/rt/dist/scripts/d-js-one-runtime-unified-desktop.min.js; 64 references to website-editor.net (static-cdn + le-cdn asset CDNs); Duda runtime globals window.rtCommonProps and dmAPI; productId: 'DM_DIRECT'; Duda site ID e64cb898a4f84235bf192060ea474fc6 in all asset paths; response headers Server: nginx with X-WS-* rate-limit headers, Speculation-Rules from static-cdn.website-editor.net, and CSP frame-ancestors allowing *.ionos.* / *.website-editor.net / *.mywebsite-editor.com (the IONOS/Duda editor domains). One reference to app.multiscreenstore.com (Duda's native store module).
scripts: Google Tag Manager — container GTM-W93L9XK5 (inline snippet + noscript iframe on every page)

Inside GTM container: Google Ads conversion tracking AW-822632954 with 3 conversion labels (PiIkCLqj_6YaEPrDoYgD, ZEZfCLSj_6YaEPrDoYgD, qzL5CLej_6YaEPrDoYgD) plus a conversion linker (__gclidw). NO GA4 tag exists in the container
Meta/Facebook Pixel — fbq('init','612955530110673') + PageView, with noscript fallback img to facebook.com/tr (site-wide)

NO standalone GA4 (no G-XXXX measurement ID anywhere on-page or in the GTM container; only a Duda feature flag 'sites.google.analytics.gtag' = true). Traffic stats are presumably Duda/IONOS built-in only

Acuity Scheduling — NOT an iframe embed; plain links/buttons to app.acuityscheduling.com, owner=13914499. Direct booking: schedule.php?appointmentType=51989230 and 56694430; cart links: catalog.php?action=addCart&id=1469464 and 1469467. Referenced on every page (8 refs on home, 6 on dog-day-school)
WhatsApp click-to-chat links: wa.me/447842116216 (valid) plus two MALFORMED ones — wa.me/01424300668 (landline with leading 0) and wa.me/4407842116216 (44+0) — these will not open a chat
Duda platform runtime: d-js-one-runtime-unified-desktop.min.js (the only external <script src> on the page)
No chat widget scripts (no tawk.to/Crisp/Intercom), no Hotjar, no Microsoft Clarity, no Calendly/Jotform embeds found on any sampled page
tech.seoMeta: Generally well-maintained for a builder site. Homepage: <title>The Fairy Tails K9 Centre</title>; meta description ("A team of qualified dog trainers, dog behaviourists, dog nutritionists, and dog groomers is based in Hastings, East Sussex."); legacy meta keywords tag; canonical https://www.thefairytails.co.uk/; full og: set (type/url/title/description/image) and twitter:card/title/description/image. CAVEAT: og:image and twitter:image point to a SIGNED, EXPIRING le-cdn.website-editor.net URL (Expires≈July 2026) — link previews will break on rebuild/expiry. Schema.org JSON-LD on homepage only: (1) WebSite and (2) LocalBusiness {name "The Fairy Tails", addressLocality Hastings, postalCode TN35 5DT, geo 50.8708/0.62896, tel 01424300668, email info@thefairytails.co.uk, sameAs WhatsApp + facebook.com/thefairytails} — but streetAddress is junk ("The Fairy Tails") and no openingHours/priceRange; subpages have NO JSON-LD. Subpages each have unique titles + meta descriptions (verified on contact, dog-day-school, dog-boarding-school, intensive-dog-training, gallery, blog). sitemap.xml present (32 URLs, with lastmod/priority). robots.txt present, fully permissive (User-agent: * with no Disallow) and declares the sitemap.
tech.notes: Rebuild-relevant findings: (1) 32 URLs total — 13 core pages (priority 1.0) + 19 blog posts (priority 0.8); all must get 301s or recreations to preserve SEO. (2) /puppy-classes is PASSWORD-PROTECTED (Duda page lock, 8.8 KB stub) yet listed in the sitemap; /internal-management-resources is a back-office page also publicly listed in the sitemap — both should be excluded/noindexed in the rebuild. (3) Booking is pure Acuity deep-links (owner 13914499, appointment types 51989230 & 56694430, cart product IDs 1469464 & 1469467) — no embed code to migrate, just preserve the URLs. (4) Tracking to carry over: GTM-W93L9XK5 (Google Ads AW-822632954 conversions only) and Meta Pixel 612955530110673; there is NO GA4 — adding one is a quick win during rebuild. (5) Two of the three WhatsApp links are malformed (wa.me/01424300668, wa.me/4407842116216) — fix to wa.me/447842116216 format. (6) All images live on Duda's le-cdn/cdn.website-editor.net with signed expiring URLs — every asset must be downloaded and re-hosted before the Duda/IONOS subscription lapses. (7) Heavy cross-linking to sister site fairytailsdoggrooming.co.uk (8 links on home) and one to adventure-dog.co.uk; socials: facebook.com/thefairytails, Instagram. (8) Homepage HTML is ~189 KB single document with inline Duda runtime config; lastmod dates show the home page (2025-12-26) and dog-boarding-school (2025-12-18) are the most recently edited pages. Fetched files for reference: C:\Users\FT Manager\AppData\Local\Temp\ft_home.html and ft_<page>.html, plus gtm.js (the GTM container).
